items.title: Warriors rookie Yaxel Lendeborg named Summer League MVP, All Summer-League teams announced
items.summary: Yaxel Lendeborg was one of the breakout stars of Summer League. He looked like a hand-in-glove fit for the Warriors, capped off by him dropping 21 and 10 in the championship game to lead Golden State to the Summer League crown.Yaxel Lendeborg, the 11th overall pick, wins the 2026 NBA Summer League Championship Game MVP! 21 PTS 10 REB 2 STL WARRIORS W pic.twitter.com/bSa4XbBBi2— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026That capped off a run that saw Lendeborg named Las Vegas Summer League MVP. Over the course of six games he averaged 14.8 points, 6.8 rebounds and four assists per game.The NBA also released the All-Summer-League teams:First TeamCameron Boozer (Memphis)Brayden Burries (Milwaukee)Yaxel Lendeborg (Golden State)Meleek Thomas (Cleveland)Caleb Wilson (Chicago)Second TeamCedric Coward (Memphis)Egor Demin (Brooklyn)Khaman Maluach (Phoenix)Liam McNeeley (Charlotte)Zyon Pullin (Minnesota)It's…

items.title: Stephen Curry becomes first current player to receive exhibit at the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame
items.summary: Golden State Warriors legend Stephen Curry isn't eligible for the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame just yet … but he's going to be honored by the establishment anyway. Curry will become the first-ever current player to receive his own exhibit at the Hall of Fame, it announced Tuesday. The exhibit, titled "Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc," will focus on — of course — Curry's prowess from 3-point land. Fans hoping to see the exhibit won't have to wait long, as it opens July 24.He inspired a generation to dream bigger and shoot farther. Now, for the first time in its nearly 75-year history, the Hall of Fame will feature a dedicated experience honoring an active NBA player. “Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc” - a limited time only exhibit opens Friday,… pic.twitter.com/7jbbhYc0x5— Basketball HOF (@Hoophall) July 21, 2026The exhibit is quite the honor, as no other current…

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg named MVP, Cameron Boozer, Caleb Wilson make NBA All-Summer League first team
items.summary: On the heels of a loaded NBA Draft, there was heightened interest in this year's Summer League. It didn't disappoint. Some of the top names from June's draft put up tantalizing performances in Las Vegas, and the NBA honored them Tuesday by announcing an MVP and All-Summer League Teams.Yaxel Lendeborg (14.8 ppg, 6.8 rpg, 4 apg, 2 spg, 35.7% from 3), who was named MVP of the championship game after leading the Golden State Warriors past Cameron Boozer and the Memphis Grizzlies, earned top honors as MVP of the entire league. Lendeborg outplayed Boozer down the stretch of a tight title game to secure the win and MVP honors. He earned the hardware months after helping lead Michigan to an NCAA championship.Boozer (18.5 ppg, 8 rpg, 3.7 apg, 1.8 spg, 33.3% from 3), who delivered with consistent production throughout the league, was named to the All-Summer League first team. As was…

items.title: Hawks trade Zaccharie Risacher, best free agents available + New York Liberty check-in
items.summary: Subscribe to The Dunker SpotApple Podcasts | Spotify | YouTubeWe have a fun episode of The Dunker Spot coming your way!First, Nekias Duncan and Steve Jones catch up on the latest transaction news in the NBA, headlined by the three-team deal that sent former #1 pick Zaccharie Risacher to the Dallas Mavericks and Lu Dort to the Atlanta Hawks. They then discuss the most interesting free agents available and their ideal fits.On the WNBA side, the guys discuss the historic pace the Indiana Fever's offense is on, then have a nuanced conversation about the New York Liberty. How much of the Liberty's season is salvageable?They close with an All-Star discussion: grading the draft between Team Coop and Team Spoon, sharing the lineups and matchups they're most excited about, and debating who should be the Kelsey Plum replacement.If you ever have NBA or WNBA questions, email us at…

items.title: Rich Paul updates timeframe for LeBron James’ free-agency decision
items.summary: The timeframe for when LeBron James will make his free agency decision does not appear to be imminent.James’ longtime friend and agent, Rich Paul of Klutch Sports, said Monday evening on the latest episode of his podcast “Game Over” with co-host Max Kellerman that they’re not going to be rushed in making a decision.“He has a choice to make. He has a business choice to make,” Paul said. “We’re not going to sit here and decide or allow someone else to decide when he makes that decision, or choice. It’s a business choice he’s making.”Rich Paul speaking with LeBron James during a Lakers game. Getty ImagesIt’s been three weeks since James informed the Los Angeles Lakers that they can move on without him next season, as he has become a free agent for the first time in eight years.James has been under heavy scrutiny, as many believe he is taking too long to decide where he will play next.“From…
